findings.comment: The iteration claims all pin surfaces moved to Spec-Kit 0.12.9 and Squad 0.11.0, but extension.yml still advertises config.defaults.versions min_speckit 0.8.4 and min_squad 0.9.1. FR-038 explicitly includes extension.yml requires/min_speckit among the surfaces that must agree, and T003 claims validate/default Squad pin parity. Update these extension defaults (and any mirror/tests that assert them) or record a human-visible deferral; as-is the completed task/progress state is false and downstream consumers can still read the old floor.

The annotation checker accepts the token anywhere on the hit line or previous line and only checks for non-empty trailing text. FR-037 requires file-kind comment syntax (HTML comment for .md, # comment for .ps1/.psd1/.yml). A targeted fixture with a bare Markdown prose line `specrew-self-ok: ...` above `FakeGallery` exits 0, so authors can bypass the self-leak firewall without the required auditable comment form and the paired tests do not cover this abuse path. Enforce the per-extension comment syntax before sanctioning hits and add red tests for wrong-form annotations.

T003 requires the validate-versions defaults to move to Squad 0.11.0 alongside the Spec-Kit 0.12.9 pin, but the shipped validator still defaults MinimumSpecKitVersion to 0.8.4 and MinimumSquadVersion to 0.9.1. The corresponding validate-versions CLI behavior tests also still assert repair/install commands at @v0.8.4, so the recorded suite evidence is not proving the new single-tested-pin behavior for this deployed validator. Update the defaults and tests to the new pins or record an explicit human-visible deferral; as-is consumers can still be told that the retired toolchain floor is acceptable.

Test-SelfLeakAnnotated accepts any line containing specrew-self-ok: with non-empty text, regardless of file extension or comment syntax. The formal SelfLeakDenyList model requires HTML comments for .md and # line comments for .ps1/.psd1/.yml, but targeted fixtures with # specrew-self-ok in templates/bad.md and HTML-comment specrew-self-ok in templates/bad.ps1 both exited 0 and sanctioned PowerShell Gallery. Enforce file-kind-specific annotation syntax before treating a deny-list hit as annotated, and add abuse tests for wrong comment forms.

The top-level extension requirement is bumped to >=0.12.9, but config.defaults.versions still advertises min_speckit 0.8.4 and min_squad 0.9.1. The formal toolchain pin contract requires extension.yml requires/min_speckit and the other pin surfaces to all agree on Spec-Kit 0.12.9 / Squad 0.11.0, so downstream consumers of this manifest can still see the retired minimums even though the shipped init path now depends on the 0.12 --integration surface.

validate-versions.ps1 still defaults MinimumSpecKitVersion to 0.8.4 and MinimumSquadVersion to 0.9.1. The formal toolchain pin contract explicitly includes validate-versions defaults in the surfaces that must all agree on 0.12.9 / 0.11.0; running this validator directly without overrides will falsely accept the retired pre-0.10 Spec Kit line even though init now uses the 0.12 --integration contract.
